pages.full_text:
was the "powder monkey", sitting on a biscuit barrel, responsible for the 14 men in his group. 8 pints beer once they were 14. 500 of the 800 men slept on one deck. 60% press ganged. No room to swing a cat.
	Then we looked at the Mary Rose being sprayed, a wax which will impregnate the wood. She should have had only 400 men, but 700 were on her the day she sank. Sir George Carew couldn't control them.
	Ropes went out the stern of the ship from a reel, showing how many "knots" the speed went.
	Shoes, clothes, medical equipment, cooking, arrows, bows, guns.
pages.notes: This is a continuation from a previous page describing a visit to the Mary Rose exhibit (Portsmouth). No date or year visible on this page. The Mary Rose museum opened 1984; this volume covers 1989-2003, so year cannot be pinned more precisely from this page alone. 'impreg-' is hyphenated across lines ('impregnate').

pages.full_text: Angels carved in bone, found in a chest on the main deck, may have formed part of the spine of a book.		Mary Rose
